goal: Give Raj a forwardable email: concrete cloud-cost levers that cut training bill ~30-50% without trashing policy quality.
thoughts: Three honest levers: (1) L40 at ~$1.10/hr often beats A100 at $2.20 for cube-lift because the task is compute-light, not VRAM-heavy; (2) num_envs sweet spot is where GPU util plateaus — profile first; (3) fewer, better-chosen seeds (3 with smart init) beats 5 random. Headless with --enable_cameras=False only if policy is state-based.
code:
# Baseline: what is current run actually bottlenecked on
profile_training_throughput(run_id="<CURRENT_RUN>")
cloud_estimate_cost(
    gpu_type="<A100_80GB>",
    hours=<18>,
    parallel_seeds=<5>
)

# Lever 1: swap instance — L40 for compute-light tasks
cloud_estimate_cost(gpu_type="<L40>", hours=<22>, parallel_seeds=<5>)

# Lever 2: tune num_envs to GPU-util plateau
launch_training(
    task="<TASK>", num_envs=<4096>,           # down from 8192 if util <85%
    headless=True, enable_cameras=<False>,    # only if policy is state-based
    log_dir="<LOG_DIR>"
)

# Lever 3: 3 seeds with smart init instead of 5 random
# Email bullets: instance swap ~50%, num_envs tune ~20%, seed pruning ~40%.
failure_modes: Disabling cameras for a vision policy — kills learning
Recommending Colab/free tier for sustained RL — session caps
Quoting fake-precision dollar savings ($312.57) instead of ranges
Suggesting L4 (24GB) for task needing 40GB+ VRAM
